## CONSULTATION HANDOFF

```
change_type: Add Feature
feature_name: multi-ai-support
date: 2026-05-18
advisor: [@devstarter-techlead, @devstarter-ba]
```

---

### Section 1 — Problem / Request

**What the user described:**
```
DevStarter is built for Claude Code but users want to use it with other AIs
(e.g., Codex, Gemini, Copilot). How to improve the project to support other AIs?
```

**Change type detected:**
- [x] Add Feature — new capability that doesn't exist yet

---

### Section 2 — Analysis Summary

**Situation understood:**
```
DevStarter is ~60% Claude Code-coupled, 40% provider-agnostic.
Core coupling points:
1. Slash commands (SKILL.md) — Claude Code exclusive entry points
2. ~/.claude/ hardcoded in install.sh, setup.sh, update.sh
3. Hooks system (SessionStart/PostToolUse/Stop) — Claude Code lifecycle only
4. CLAUDE.md convention — Claude Code startup context file

Already abstracted (no changes needed):
- AI provider config in devstarter-config.yml (claude|litellm|openai|gemini|local)
- VCS abstraction (github/gitlab/svn)
- PM tools (notion/jira/github-issues/linear/trello)
- LiteLLM proxy docs in templates/litellm/
```

**Root cause / Gap:**
```
Non-Claude users have no entry point into DevStarter workflows.
They can't invoke /devstarter-* commands. There are no adapter guides,
no "Universal Prompt" blocks in SKILL.md files, and no provider-detect
install logic. The SDLC content is provider-neutral prose — just needs
a different delivery wrapper.
```

**Affected area / files (if identified):**
```
Phase 1 (adapter guides):
- skills/devstarter-*/SKILL.md (43 files — add Universal Prompt block)
- README.md (add multi-AI section)
- docs/multi-ai-guide.md (new file)
- devstarter-config.yml (expand ai.provider enum)

Phase 2 (install abstraction):
- install.sh (provider-detect, install to ~/.{provider}/)
- update.sh, uninstall.sh (same)
- templates/CLAUDE.md.template → PROJECT.md.template
- scripts/devstarter-config-hook.sh (conditional on Claude Code)
```

---

### Section 3 — Recommended Approach

**Recommended option from consultation:**
```
Option A → Option B (staged):
Phase 1: Documentation Adapter — add Universal Prompt blocks to SKILL.md files,
         create docs/multi-ai-guide.md for Codex + Gemini + Copilot.
Phase 2: Provider-Detect Install — modify install.sh to detect AI_PROVIDER,
         generate PROJECT.md alongside CLAUDE.md, add devstarter-invoke.sh.
```

**Why this was recommended:**
```
The SDLC runbooks and agent files are already provider-neutral prose.
Don't rewrite content — wrap the delivery. Phase 1 ships fast with zero
risk to existing Claude users. Phase 2 only if Phase 1 gets traction.
Skip LiteLLM-as-middleware (Option C) — adds infra complexity for
uncertain ROI.
```

**Estimated effort:** M (Phase 1: S ~2 days / Phase 2: M ~1-2 weeks)

**Risks or caveats:**
```
- Each AI has different context injection mechanisms — need per-provider research
- Hooks (session memory, auto-sync) won't work on non-Claude AIs — document as limitation
- 43 SKILL.md files need Universal Prompt blocks — repetitive but mechanical work
- Phase 2 install abstraction must not break existing Claude Code users
```

---

### Section 4 — Acceptance Criteria

**Done when (Phase 1):**
```
Given a user on Codex/Gemini/Copilot with DevStarter installed for Claude
When they open docs/multi-ai-guide.md
Then they can copy a Universal Prompt and invoke any DevStarter workflow
     in their AI tool without needing Claude Code
```

**Done when (Phase 2):**
```
Given AI_PROVIDER=codex set in environment
When user runs bash install.sh
Then DevStarter installs to ~/.codex/ and generates a PROJECT.md context file
     alongside CLAUDE.md, with hooks skipped for non-Claude providers
```

**Regression guard (must still work):**
```
- All existing Claude Code slash commands (/devstarter-*) continue working
- install.sh with no AI_PROVIDER defaults to Claude (~/.claude/) behavior unchanged
- devstarter-config.yml ai.provider: claude still routes to Claude models
```

---

Problem:
  DevStarter is Claude Code-only; users on Codex/Gemini/Copilot
  have no way to invoke DevStarter workflows.

Root cause / Gap:
  Delivery layer is Claude-coupled (slash commands, ~/.claude/ paths,
  hooks). Core SDLC content is already provider-neutral.

Recommended:
  Phase 1 — Adapter Guides (v4.7.0): Universal Prompt blocks in
  SKILL.md + docs/multi-ai-guide.md. Effort: S
  Phase 2 — Install Abstraction (v5.0.0): provider-detect install.sh
  + PROJECT.md generation + devstarter-invoke.sh. Effort: M

Acceptance criteria:
  Phase 1: Non-Claude user can invoke any workflow via copy-paste prompt
  Phase 2: install.sh installs to correct ~/.{provider}/ path automatically
